news.summary: According to analysts at Bernstein, as trust in centralized institutions diminishes globally, three key decentralized technologies - Bitcoin (CRYPTO: BTC), public blockchains, and tokenized assets - are predicted to play a crucial role in shaping a new financial system. The report from Bernstein's digital assets team highlights that factors such as geopolitical shifts, trade disputes, and fragmented financial systems are driving the adoption of decentralized technologies among both sovereign entities and institutional players. Lead analyst Gautam Chhugani emphasized that Bitcoin, decentralized blockchains, and tokenized currencies are establishing a foundation for a new settlement and value framework. The report underscores Bitcoin's growing reputation as a geopolitically independent digital store of value. With a market cap of approximately $2 trillion, Bitcoin is positioned... (to read the full story, visit Benzinga.com)
news.headline: Analysts at Bernstein believe Bitcoin, blockchains, and tokenized assets will shape the new financial infrastructure. Bitcoin holds $2 trillion market cap, seen as a geopolitical-free store of value. - Benzinga.

news.summary: In Atlanta, DS Smith, a prominent fiber-based packaging maker acquired by International Paper in 2025, has introduced a new cooler that is both 100% recyclable and reusable. Named the "GoChill Cooler", this eco-friendly alternative to plastic and Styrofoam coolers is made from wax-free corrugated board. The cooler aims to offer consumers a sustainable solution for keeping food and drinks fresh while reducing waste and carbon emissions. Notably, this moisture-resistant design emphasizes the company's commitment to environmental responsibility.
news.headline: DS Smith launches recyclable, reusable cooler to replace Styrofoam, reducing waste & carbon emissions.

news.summary: UiPath, Inc. (NYSE: PATH) stocks are up today as the company announced the introduction of its UiPath Medical Record Summarization agent during the Google Cloud Next 2025 event. This new agent utilizes generative AI technology and is powered by Google Cloud Vertex AI and Gemini models. It is currently in a private preview phase and aims to assist payer and provider organizations in enhancing the efficiency and accuracy of medical record analysis. Developed in collaboration with clinical professionals, the agent provides detailed summaries of medical documents at a clinician level, completing the process in minutes as opposed to the usual 45 minutes it takes manually.
news.headline: UiPath introduces AI Medical Record Summarization solution at Google Cloud Next 2025, reduces processing time from 45 mins to minutes.

news.summary: In Pittsburgh on April 9, 2025, an inventor from Asheville, N.C. shared their motivation for creating a new product. The inventor expressed concern after almost colliding with pedestrians twice while driving, leading them to develop an umbrella designed to enhance user visibility during poor weather conditions or at night. This innovation emphasizes the importance of pedestrian safety, with the hope of reducing accidents and promoting awareness on the roads.

news.summary: In Santa Rosa, California, Exchange Bank (OTC: EXSR) has partnered with the Federal Home Loan Bank of San Francisco to provide a substantial $800,000 grant to the Dry Creek Rancheria Band of Pomo Indians for enhancing affordable housing development capacity. news.summary: According to BofA Securities analyst Wamsi Mohan, he reiterated a Buy rating on Apple Inc (NASDAQ: AAPL) with a target price of $250. The discussion has shifted towards the potential of producing Apple products like iPhones in the U.S. Mohan highlighted that while assembling iPhones in the U.S. is feasible in terms of labor, a considerable portion of the sub-assemblies would still be made elsewhere, assembled in China, and brought to the U.S. Analysts have speculated that shifting the entire iPhone supply chain to the U.S. would be a significant and lengthy process. Mohan provided estimates on how the bill of materials (BOM) cost for an iPhone 16 Pro Max would increase if only the final assembly was moved to the U.S., factoring in potential tariffs. As per the analyst, iPhone costs could surge by 25% due to increased labor expenses. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Analyst predicts Apple may face up to 90% cost surge on iPhones with US tariffs, considering move to India, 25% increase possible from labor costs alone.

news.summary: In Pittsburgh on April 9, 2025, an inventor from Warilla, NSW, Australia, shared the creation of the P T SCORER, a new tool designed to simplify score tracking and serving for pickleball players. This innovative solution aims to eliminate scoring confusion and delays during matches. The inventor sought to enhance the player experience by providing a convenient way to manage scores and serve efficiently.
news.headline: Inventor from Australia develops P T SCORER to simplify pickleball scoring and serving.

news.summary: The price of Bitcoin (BTC) surged past $82,000 on Wednesday after a major short squeeze resulted in the liquidation of $75 million worth of bearish bets in just one hour. Bitcoin reached a high of $82,450, as reported by TradingView data. This surge followed President Trump's announcement of a tariff increase on China to 125%, with an immediate effect, while pausing reciprocal tariffs on 75 countries for 90 days. Altcoins also experienced significant gains during this period. Ethereum (ETH) increased by 9%, Ripple's (XRP) rose by 10%, Solana (SOL) jumped by 12%, and Dogecoin (DOGE) added 10%. The market reaction was notable and further details can be found on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Bitcoin surged to $82,000 due to short squeeze, liquidating $75M in bearish bets. Trump announced a 90-day pause on reciprocal tariffs for all countries except China. Altcoins like Ethereum, Ripple, Solana, and Dogecoin also experienced significant gains.

news.summary: In a recent report, it was revealed that the number of unemployed individuals in the country has risen to 8.4 million, marking a significant increase from the previous month. This surge in unemployment has caused the national unemployment rate to climb to 6.7%. Additionally, the report highlighted a decrease in job creation, with only 245,000 new jobs added last month, falling short of the expected 350,000. The current economic climate continues to be challenging, with the labor market facing ongoing struggles amid the pandemic. Experts are closely monitoring these developments to assess the long-term impact on the economy and employment landscape.
news.headline: Court allows Trump to enforce firing of employees at 20 agencies - source

news.summary: The article discusses the increasing trade tensions and policy uncertainty under the Trump administration, along with the Federal Reserve's response. Craig Shapiro from 3-Circle Investments warns that the absence of the perceived "Trump put" or "Fed put" could lead to a significant market downturn. This means that investors are unsure if the government or the Fed will step in to stabilize the markets during times of crisis. Shapiro believes that the Fed will only intervene if the U.S. Treasury market faces serious issues. Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell reiterated that the Fed's priority is to achieve maximum employment and stable prices, but they are closely monitoring the economic impact of new policies, especially trade. This uncertainty could potentially drive the markets lower.
news.headline: Market could drop without 'Trump put' or 'Fed put,' says expert as Fed Chair Powell holds firm on rate cuts. Powell emphasizes maintaining labor market and stable prices amid trade tensions. Fed may intervene if U.S. Treasury market falters.

news.summary: Guardian Capital LP made an announcement on March 18, 2025, stating that the distribution for Guardian Ultra-Short U.S. T-Bill Fund (TSX: GUTB.U) was incorrectly reported in Canadian dollars. The distribution per ETF unit of the Fund should have been reported in U.S. dollars, with the corrected amount being USD$0.1725. Despite the correction, the ex-dividend date, record date, and payment date for the distribution will remain unchanged.
news.headline: Guardian Capital corrects March 2025 cash distribution for Guardian Ultra-Short U.S. T-Bill Fund to USD$0.1725 per ETF unit.

news.summary: U.S. stock futures faced a sharp decline on Monday with major indices dropping over 3% in premarket trading. The selloff intensified after China introduced retaliatory measures to U.S. tariffs. As of Friday, the S&P 500 was down 17.46% from its record high, nearing bear market territory. The Dow was 14.99% lower from its 52-week high, and the Nasdaq 100 had already entered bear market territory, down 21.71%. Jerome Powell's uncertain comments on monetary policy added to market volatility. The 10-year Treasury bond yielded 3.93%. Futures indicated significant drops: Dow Jones -3.61%, S&P 500 -3.74%, Nasdaq 100 -4.26%, Russell 2000 -4.82%. ETFs tracking these indices also fell in premarket trading. Economic data showed strong job growth in March but also an increase in unemployment. The S&P 500 saw its worst weekly decline since March 2020. Experts highlighted worrying similarities with patterns seen during the Great Depression in the market's recent behavior.
news.headline: US stock futures plummet by over 3% following tariff-induced selloff, nearing bear market territory. S&P 500 down 17.46% and Dow 14.99% from their highs. Experts warn of 'Great Depression-like' pattern. Fed considering rate cuts, bond yields at 3.93% and 3.51%. Indices decline further on Monday.

news.summary: The management board of AS PRFoods, with registration code 11560713 and address at Pärnu mnt 141, Tallinn 11314, has suggested to shareholders the adoption of resolutions without the need for a meeting, as per § 299 1 of the Commercial Code. The company's ISIN is EE3100101031.
news.headline: AS PRFoods proposes adoption of resolutions without meeting via Commercial Code §299 1. Registration code: 11560713, ISIN: EE3100101031.
